questions.text: How comfortable are you saying 'I love you' first?

options.label: Very – I say it when I feel it
Somewhat – if the moment feels right
Prefer to wait for them to say it first
Very cautious – need to be absolutely sure

questions.text: You're about to sleep with someone for the first time. Do you leave the lights on?

options.label: All the way on
Dimmed or candles
Off completely
Whatever they prefer

questions.text: How important is physical chemistry on a first date?

options.label: Crucial – I need to feel that spark immediately
Important, but can develop over time
Somewhat important, but personality matters more
Not very – I need emotional connection first

questions.text: When do you typically feel ready for physical intimacy?

options.label: When there's strong mutual attraction
After a few great dates and conversations
Once emotional connection is established
Within a committed, exclusive relationship

questions.text: How do you feel about sleeping over on the first date?

options.label: If the vibe is right, why not?
Open to it but no expectations
Prefer to wait a bit
Definitely not – too soon

questions.text: How do you feel about public displays of affection?

options.label: Love it – I'm naturally affectionate
Enjoy it in moderation (hand-holding, light kisses)
Prefer to keep it minimal in public
Uncomfortable with PDA, prefer private affection

questions.text: Do you prefer to be the big spoon or little spoon?

options.label: Big spoon
Little spoon
Switch it up
I need my space when I sleep

questions.text: Would you be willing to try role-playing in bed?

options.label: Yes, sounds fun!
Maybe with the right person
Probably not my thing
No, I'd feel too awkward

questions.text: How do you feel about morning breath kisses?

options.label: Totally fine, comes with the territory
Okay once we're really comfortable
Please brush your teeth first
Absolutely not

questions.text: Would you date someone who was in a committed non-monogamous relationship?

options.label: Yes, I'm open to that
Maybe, depending on the situation
Probably not, but I'm not judging
No, I need monogamy

questions.text: Should your partner be your best friend?

options.label: Absolutely yes
Ideally, but not required
No, I keep friendship and romance separate
I'm not sure

questions.text: How would you feel if your partner wanted to shower together regularly?

options.label: Love it, sounds intimate
Sure, occasionally
I need my shower time alone
Logistically impractical

questions.text: How important is it that you and your partner have similar sex drives?

options.label: Extremely important – dealbreaker level
Very important but we can compromise
Somewhat important
Not that important

questions.text: Would you rather:

options.label: Have amazing sex with someone you barely know
Have okay sex with someone you deeply love
These aren't mutually exclusive
I refuse to choose

questions.text: You're getting intimate and your partner says 'I love you' for the first time. You don't feel the same yet. You:

options.label: Say it back to make them feel good
Say something like 'that means so much'
Be honest that I'm not there yet
This scenario is my nightmare

questions.text: How do you feel about farting in front of your partner?

options.label: Natural and funny, no big deal
Eventually it happens
I'd be mortified
Never. Some mystery is good.

questions.text: Your ideal date night involves:

options.label: Adventure and trying something new together
Romantic dinner and intimate conversation
Cozy night in with movies or cooking
Cultural experience like a concert or museum

questions.text: What does intimacy mean to you beyond physical connection?

options.label: Complete emotional vulnerability and openness
Deep understanding of each other's inner worlds
Unwavering trust and reliability
Accepting each other fully, flaws and all

questions.text: Would you ask for a brief back massage on your second date (shirt on)?

options.label: Yes, absolutely – physical touch builds connection
Maybe, if the vibe feels right
Probably not – too forward for a second date
No – I'd wait until we're more comfortable

questions.text: For you personally, is sex one of the most important parts of a relationship?

options.label: Yes, it's essential for connection
Very important but not the most important
Somewhat important
Not very important

questions.text: Are you dominant or submissive in the bedroom?

options.label: I am vanilla
I am submissive
I am a switch
I am dominant

questions.text: It would bother me if my partner consumed porn.

options.label: Strongly agree – it would bother me a lot
Somewhat agree – I'd prefer they didn't
Neutral – it depends on the context
Somewhat disagree – doesn't bother me much
Strongly disagree – doesn't bother me at all

questions.text: What's your stance on wanting children?

options.label: Definitely want children
Open to it with the right partner
Unsure – could go either way
Have kid(s) already but don't want more
Definitely don't want children

questions.text: Would you date someone who has a kid?

options.label: Yes, no problem
Depends on the situation
Probably not
Definitely not

questions.text: Are you willing to date someone with a disability?

options.label: Yes, absolutely
Yes, it depends on the disability and our compatibility
I'm not sure / I'd have to think about it
Probably not

questions.text: How do you approach finances in a relationship?

options.label: Completely joint – what's mine is ours
Mostly shared with some personal accounts
Split expenses but keep finances separate
Completely independent financial lives

questions.text: Should the man always pay on dates?

options.label: Yes, traditionally
On the first date, then split
Split everything equally
Whoever asks should pay, regardless of gender

questions.text: Would you consider relocating for a partner's job?

options.label: Absolutely, if we're serious
Yes, depending on the circumstances
Maybe if we're married or engaged
No, my career/location is too important

questions.text: How do you feel about living together before marriage?

options.label: Absolutely necessary to test compatibility
Prefer it but not required
Open to it but would rather wait
Prefer to wait until marriage

questions.text: How do you feel about your partner having close friends of the opposite sex?

options.label: Totally fine – trust is everything
Okay with it but appreciate transparency
Somewhat uncomfortable but willing to work through it
Prefer clear boundaries in this area

questions.text: How do you handle jealousy in relationships?

options.label: I don't really get jealous – I trust easily
Occasional jealousy but I communicate about it
Sometimes struggle with it but work on it
Tend to be protective of relationships

questions.text: How many people have you slept with?

options.label: More than 25 / None of your business

questions.text: Would you ever do a threesome?

options.label: Yes, I'd be interested
Maybe with the right people
Probably not but never say never
Definitely not my thing

questions.text: What are you ultimately looking for?

options.label: Life partner and eventual marriage
Long-term committed relationship
Open to seeing where things go naturally
Casual dating and companionship

questions.text: How long do you typically date before becoming exclusive?

options.label: 1-2 dates if there's strong chemistry
A few weeks to a month
2-3 months of consistent dating
Several months – I take my time
I don't want to be in an exclusive relationship

questions.text: How do you feel about marriage?

options.label: Definitely want to get married
Open to it with the right person
Unsure – don't need marriage to commit
Don't believe in marriage

questions.text: Would you stay in a relationship if you were no longer in love?

options.label: Never – love is essential
Maybe if there were other factors (kids, etc.)
Yes, love changes over time
I'm not sure

questions.text: Could you forgive infidelity?

options.label: Never – immediate dealbreaker
Probably not but depends on circumstances
Maybe with couples therapy
Yes, people make mistakes

questions.text: How long was your longest relationship?

options.label: Never been in a relationship
Less than 6 months
6 months to 2 years
2-5 years
More than 5 years

questions.text: What relationship style are you most interested in?

options.label: Monogamy – exclusive with one person
Open relationship – committed but sexually open
Polyamory – multiple romantic relationships
Relationship anarchy – no hierarchies or rules
Exploring/Not sure yet

questions.text: How do you feel about non-monogamy?

options.label: Not for me – I prefer exclusive relationships
Open to discussing with the right person
Interested but haven't explored it
Experienced and prefer ethical non-monogamy

questions.text: Would you be comfortable with your partner having other romantic connections?

options.label: No – I need complete romantic exclusivity
Maybe sexual but not romantic connections
Yes, with clear communication and boundaries
Yes, and I prefer relationship autonomy

questions.text: How do you feel about long distance relationships?

options.label: Open to it – distance doesn't matter with the right person
Maybe for the right person, temporarily
Prefer not to but would consider it
Absolutely not – need physical proximity

questions.text: Does emotional entanglement count as cheating?

options.label: Yes, absolutely – emotional cheating is still cheating
It depends on the situation and context
Not really – cheating is only physical
I'm not sure what counts as emotional entanglement

questions.text: How do you feel about kissing on a first date?

options.label: Absolutely – if the chemistry is there, go for it
Maybe a quick kiss if it feels right
Prefer to wait until the second or third date
I'd rather wait until we're more serious

questions.text: How important is it that your partner initiates intimacy?

options.label: Very – I prefer when they make the first move
Somewhat – I like a balance of both initiating
Not very – I'm usually the one to initiate
Doesn't matter – either way works for me

questions.text: How do you prefer to have 'the talk' about becoming official?

options.label: Direct conversation – I bring it up clearly
Wait for the other person to bring it up
Let it happen naturally without a formal talk
I avoid defining relationships if possible

questions.text: How do you feel about dating apps?

options.label: Love them – it's how I meet most people
Useful but I prefer meeting people organically
Only use them occasionally
Avoid them – I prefer real-life connections

questions.text: How do you feel about your partner following their ex on social media?

options.label: Totally fine – the past is the past
Okay but I'd want to know about it
Would prefer they didn't but won't demand it
Uncomfortable with it – I'd want them to unfollow

questions.text: How do you feel about getting engaged before living together?

options.label: Traditional and romantic – I like the idea
Either way is fine with me
Prefer to live together first
Absolutely must live together before engagement

archetypes.name: The Hopeless Romantic
archetypes.description: You believe in love at first sight and aren't afraid to dive in headfirst. You're affectionate, expressive, and view relationships as the center of a fulfilling life.
suggestions: Balance romantic ideals with realistic expectations
Allow relationships time to develop naturally
Find a partner who matches your enthusiasm and isn't scared by intensity

archetypes.name: The Thoughtful Partner
archetypes.description: You approach relationships with intention and care. You value deep connection, open communication, and building something meaningful over time.
suggestions: Don't overthink – sometimes it's okay to just enjoy the moment
Trust your instincts alongside your analysis
Find someone who appreciates depth and is ready for emotional intimacy

archetypes.name: The Independent Spirit
archetypes.description: You value your autonomy and bring a sense of self to your relationships. You're looking for a partner who enhances your life without defining it.
suggestions: Remember to make time for vulnerability and intimacy
Let your partner in when it feels right
Find someone confident who has their own full life

archetypes.name: The Adventure Seeker
archetypes.description: You want a partner to explore life with. You value spontaneity, new experiences, and a relationship that feels exciting and dynamic.
suggestions: Don't forget to create stability amidst the excitement
Build deeper roots while staying adventurous
Find someone who's up for anything and shares your zest for life

archetypes.name: The Slow Burn
archetypes.description: You believe the best relationships develop gradually. You need time to build trust and prefer friendship as the foundation of romance.
suggestions: Be open to unexpected connections that don't fit your timeline
Sometimes chemistry arrives before comfort
Find someone patient who understands that good things take time

archetypes.name: The Practical Partner
archetypes.description: You approach relationships with your head and your heart. You value compatibility, shared goals, and building a life together that makes sense.
suggestions: Leave room for passion and spontaneity
Sometimes the heart knows what the head can't calculate
Find someone who shares your life vision and values

archetypes.name: The Free Spirit
archetypes.description: You're open to wherever life takes you. You don't believe in forcing relationships and prefer to let things unfold naturally without pressure.
suggestions: Communicate your needs even when going with the flow
Be clear about what you're looking for to avoid mismatched expectations
Find someone who doesn't need labels or timelines

archetypes.name: The Balanced One
archetypes.description: You've found equilibrium between independence and partnership, passion and stability, planning and spontaneity. You bring well-rounded energy to relationships.
suggestions: Don't be afraid to lean into your preferences more strongly
Your balance is a strength – use it to navigate relationship challenges
Find someone who appreciates your balanced approach

archetypes.name: The Protector
archetypes.description: You show love through acts of service and making your partner feel safe. You're reliable, consistent, and express affection by taking care of the people you love.
suggestions: Remember to let others take care of you too – receiving is also an act of love
Balance protection with allowing your partner independence
Find someone who appreciates being cared for and reciprocates in their own way

archetypes.name: The Passionate Lover
archetypes.description: Physical touch is your primary love language. You crave intimacy and closeness, expressing and feeling love most deeply through physical presence.
suggestions: Develop other ways to connect when physical touch isn't possible
Communicate that physical touch is your love language
Find someone equally comfortable with physical intimacy and touch

archetypes.name: The Devotee
archetypes.description: When you love, you love completely. You're all-in, prioritizing your relationship and your partner above most other things. Your loyalty is unshakeable.
suggestions: Maintain your individual identity and friendships outside the relationship
Ensure your devotion is reciprocated
Find someone who won't take advantage of your devotion and reciprocates fully

archetypes.name: The Communicator
archetypes.description: Words are your love language. You need to hear 'I love you,' crave meaningful conversations, and express your feelings verbally. Silent love doesn't register for you.
suggestions: Learn to recognize love expressed through actions, not just words
Be patient with partners who show love differently
Find someone who expresses their feelings openly and enjoys talking

archetypes.name: The Quality Timer
archetypes.description: Undivided attention is how you give and receive love. You crave meaningful time together without distractions. Being truly present is the greatest gift to you.
suggestions: Understand that some people show love through busy schedules juggling responsibilities
Communicate your need for dedicated quality time
Find someone who puts away their phone and gives you their full attention

archetypes.name: The Playful Partner
archetypes.description: Laughter and fun are at the heart of your relationships. You keep things light, enjoy teasing, and believe couples who play together stay together.
suggestions: Be willing to engage with serious topics when necessary
Balance playfulness with emotional depth
Find someone with a great sense of humor who doesn't take themselves too seriously

archetypes.name: The Soulmate Seeker
archetypes.description: You're searching for 'the one' – that deep, cosmic connection that feels fated. You believe in destiny, twin flames, and love that transcends the ordinary.
suggestions: Great relationships are built, not just found – even soulmates require work
Stay open to connections that don't feel instantly cosmic
Find someone who shares your belief in deeper meaning and spiritual connection

archetypes.name: The Secure Attacher
archetypes.description: You have a healthy relationship with intimacy and independence. You're comfortable with closeness and can handle separation without anxiety. You communicate needs clearly.
suggestions: Your security is a gift – use it to help partners feel safe too
Be patient with partners who have different attachment styles
You can adapt to different styles – find someone who values healthy communication

archetypes.name: The Traditionalist
archetypes.description: You value classic romance and traditional relationship milestones. Marriage, family, and building a conventional life together are important to you.
suggestions: Stay open to partners whose timelines differ from yours
Traditional values can coexist with modern flexibility
Find someone who shares your vision of traditional commitment and family

archetypes.name: The Modern Lover
archetypes.description: You reject traditional relationship scripts and create your own rules. You're open to non-traditional arrangements and believe love should be defined by those in it.
suggestions: Communicate your preferences clearly – don't assume others share your flexibility
Be upfront about your relationship style from the start
Find someone equally flexible about relationship definitions and expectations
